other_observers: Glenn Walbek, Steve Larson, Lisa Edwards, MOB
specific_location: Below the dam at Two Buttes Res
description: The bird was a yellow type Dendroica type warbler, with white wing patches and black streaked back. Eye and bill were dark. The rufous auriculars were surrounded below by bright yellow and on top a darker crown. The yellow extended onto the back of the neck and into the lores. The yellow breast and flanks had black streaks. The vent area was clear and yellowish.Behavior: The bird was observed fly catching over a small pond. Flying back and forth from a willow tree hanging over the pond. Occasionally, the bird would glean insects from the treeCall: none heardPlumage: breeding
